title: Zoro Stays Awake
description: On pages 13–15, zoro stays awake as after facing the Grand Line’s bizarre weather, the crew reaches Whiskey Peak and is greeted with suspicious enthusiasm.
scene: Zoro Stays Awake. After facing the Grand Line’s bizarre weather, the crew reaches Whiskey Peak and is greeted with suspicious enthusiasm. The scene focuses on the immediate character conflict rather than background exposition.

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: They're gone...

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Just what is up with those two...

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Forget about'em! Prepare to land!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: There's a river right up ahead.

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Looks like we can dock inland.

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: t-there wouldn't be any monsters on this island now, right...?

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Considering this place is Grand Line and all, I'd say there is a good possibility.

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Well, we can always run away if that's the case.

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: Wait just a second.

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: Don't forget that we have to stay on this island for a certain amount of time.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Why?

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: This log pose has to record this island's magnetic field,

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: Before we can move on to the next island!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: There're some islands that take only a few hours to record, while some take even a few days.

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: and the recording time varies for each island.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: T-then even if we come to a horrible monster island that we want to run away from,

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: It's possible that we'll have to stay on it for several days...!?

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: That's right.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: Well, we'll worry about it when it happens.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: So let's just go already!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: It'd be weird not to go in when there's a river in front waiting for us!

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: for you, maybe...

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: No, what he says is right. There's no use in worrying about it so let's just go.

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: I'll protect you, Nami!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: H-H-Hey Guys... Listen... I uh...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: I think I've suddenly developed a can't-enter- islands- disease.

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: ...Fine, let's go in then.

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: I'm serious, guys. My can't- ente...

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: Ready?

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: be prepared to fight or run at a moment's notice.

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Huh, isn't that a pirate ship?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: Hey? You're right!

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: I can hear people.

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: But this fog's too thick to see them.

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: People!? You sure it's people!?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: Go tell the others!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: pirates are here!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: Welcome!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: To the town of welcoming, whiskey peak!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: pirates!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: Welcome to our town!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: Welcome to Grand Line!

summary: This part advances the chapter by focusing on zoro stays awake. It keeps the main tension clear while preserving the dialogue that drives the scene.
